revision_reviewed: rev-5 (uncommitted edits on commit b73abd8a addressing rev-3 findings)

warnings.title: Rev-3 N3 unaddressed: removing _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS[set_source] would break test_service.py:367 with no fix-up task
warnings.evidence: Plan line 928 says 'If so, remove the entry from _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS to avoid divergence'. test_service.py:367 pins the literal string "MissingRequiredPaths" (confirmed at worktree path). service.py:2039 is the only emit site for error_class="MissingRequiredPaths" (in the _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS branch). Appendix C closure table (plan lines 1766-1772) does not list rev-3 N3 at all. If Task 4's 'if redundant' branch is taken, the test will fail on the next pytest run, and no plan step instructs the agent to fix test_service.py:367.
warnings.resolution: Either (a) add an explicit step in Task 4 to update test_service.py:367 if the entry is removed, or (b) change the conditional removal to unconditional (per rev-3 N7: 'should be unconditional — Pydantic model is strictly more capable') and track the test update. The escape hatch 'if there is a meaningful difference, document why both are kept' avoids the problem if the agent chooses to keep both, but that path must be explicitly preferred or the fix-up must be spelled out.

warnings.title: Rev-3 W1/W2 preflight test counts: rephrased but not refreshed to current figures
warnings.evidence: Rev-3 W1 asked to refresh '1565 tests' to ~15,022; rev-3 W2 asked to refresh '>=6 integration tests' to 781. Plan lines 25-26 now read 'count will be much larger than 1565; that figure is from when this plan was first drafted; verify zero failures, not exact count' and 'current count is in the hundreds; figure was drafted early; verify zero failures, not exact count'. The assertion is now 'zero failures' which is functionally adequate, but the old stale figure (1565) is retained in prose as a historical reference, and no current count is provided.
warnings.resolution: Functionally adequate as written — the gate assertion is zero failures, not an exact count. Suggest replacing the parenthetical stale figure with the current figure (15,022 unit / 781 integration as of rev-3 measurement) so operators have a sanity bound. Minor drift only.

warnings.title: service.py:1836-1870 JSON-decode gate reference: off-by-2 on start line
warnings.evidence: Plan line 1354 (Task 17) references 'JSON-decode / non-dict pre-dispatch gate at service.py:1836-1870'. Actual: the decode starts at service.py:1838 (grep confirmed); line 1836 is comment context. End line is 1881 (not 1870). The range is a documentation approximation.
warnings.resolution: Update the reference to 'service.py:1838-1881' for accuracy. Does not affect implementation correctness.

minor.title: Cross-reference 'lines 255-273 of test_walk_model_schema.py' in property test docstring cites plan-file line numbers, not generated-file line numbers
minor.evidence: Plan line 1533-1534 (inside test_redaction_completeness_property.py's docstring) says 'verified by tests at lines 255-273 of tests/unit/web/composer/test_walk_model_schema.py'. Those numbers (255-273) are plan-document line numbers, not the line numbers of the generated file. The generated test file begins at plan line 94, so the value_provider tests (plan lines 257-274) will land at approximately file lines 164-181, not 255-273. The test file does not yet exist on disk (confirmed: ls tests/unit/web/composer/ shows no test_walk_model_schema.py).
minor.evidence_detail: plan lines 257/268 are the value_provider test functions; code block starts at plan line 93; generated file line = plan_line - 93 + 1 ≈ 164/176. The docstring will contain incorrect line citations.

minor.title: redaction_telemetry.py does not yet exist; plan correctly marks it as to-be-created
minor.evidence: ls confirms src/elspeth/web/composer/redaction_telemetry.py NOT FOUND. Plan lines 44 and 47 mark it as 'Create'. No hallucination — plan correctly states file does not exist. Recorded for completeness.

minor.title: Residual silent-skip path in property test (value_provider is None guard with with_values=True)
minor.evidence: Plan line 1578: 'if node.value_provider is None: continue'. The test calls walk_model_schema(model, with_values=True), so a correctly implemented iterator always sets value_provider. However, if the implementation has a bug for a particular model shape, this guard silently skips the assertion rather than failing loudly. This is structurally weaker than asserting value_provider is not None for all Sensitive nodes. The original B1 defect was an ad-hoc parser returning None; this is a bug-masking guard on a field that should always be non-None in this call path. Less severe than B1 (does not affect all container paths), but still a quality concern outside strict reality lane.

B1_property_test_path_traversal.evidence: Plan Task 19 (lines 1519-1605) replaces the ad-hoc path.split('.') loop with node.value_provider(args) from the TraversalNode API. The property test docstring explicitly documents the fix and cites 'Closes rev-3 B1' (plan line 1534). Appendix C entry at plan line 1770 confirms closure by Task 19 value_provider-based path extraction. The value_provider closure is built by walk_model_schema and handles both flat paths and container-descent paths (items[*].secret, lookup{*}.secret) correctly — verified by tests at plan lines 257-274 (test_value_provider_handles_list_index_descent, test_value_provider_handles_dict_key_descent). A residual silent-skip guard (value_provider is None: continue) exists but is a quality concern rather than a reality defect — see R-N3.

M1_iterator_completeness_oracle.evidence: Plan Task 10 (line 1171) adds the walker completeness floor-check: 'Assert that walk_model_schema emits at least one node whose path root matches every top-level field in model_fields.' Test function test_walker_emits_node_for_every_top_level_field added to test_adequacy_guard.py (plan lines 1176-1200). Appendix C at line 1771 confirms closure. Done-when criterion at plan line 1718 explicitly checks this. The oracle uses a separate assertion (model_fields key set) rather than relying on the shared iterator's internal logic, providing the cross-cutting check that rev-3 M1 demanded.

M2_MANIFEST_membership_precondition.evidence: Plan Task 17 (lines 1362-1393) adds test_redact_tool_call_arguments_raises_for_unknown_tool to test_compose_loop_unknown_tool_name.py, mechanically pinning that redact_tool_call_arguments raises AuditIntegrityError for unknown tool names. Appendix C at line 1772 confirms closure. Done-when criterion at plan line 1719 verifies the test. The 5-line mechanical test that rev-3 M2 requested is present.

W1_stale_unit_count.evidence: Plan lines 25-26 replace the hard figure with 'count will be much larger than 1565; that figure is from when this plan was first drafted; verify zero failures, not exact count'. The assertion is now 'zero failures' (functionally adequate). Rev-3 asked to 'refresh figure to current collection count'. The stale 1565 figure is retained as a historical reference rather than replaced with the current ~15,022 figure. Raised as R-W2.

W2_stale_integration_count.evidence: Plan line 26 replaces '>=6 integration tests' with 'current count is in the hundreds; figure was drafted early; verify zero failures'. Same pattern as W1 — functionally adequate gate assertion but stale figure not refreshed to current 781. Raised as R-W2.

N3_test_service_367_pin.evidence: Appendix C (plan lines 1766-1772) lists only B1, M1, M2 as rev-3 closures. Rev-3 N3 ('_TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal changes audit error_class from MissingRequiredPaths to dispatch class; test_service.py:367 pins old string') is not listed and not addressed. test_service.py:367 still contains 'assert "MissingRequiredPaths" in result.message' (confirmed in worktree). service.py:2039 is the only emit site of error_class="MissingRequiredPaths". Plan Task 4 line 928 uses conditional 'if redundant, remove' language with no fix-up instruction for test_service.py:367. Raised as R-W1.

convention_verification.no_defensive_programming: No hasattr, getattr(x, y, default), or .get() with default found in plan code samples for non-trust-boundary code. Plan samples use direct attribute access (validated.field) and explicit Pydantic model_validate.
convention_verification.freeze_guard: TraversalNode defined as frozen=True, slots=True dataclass (plan line 396-402); fields are path:str, field_type:type, metadata:tuple[Any,...], value_provider:Callable|None. The tuple field contains Any-typed elements — freeze_fields() is not shown in __post_init__. CLAUDE.md requires freeze_fields() for frozen dataclasses with container fields. However, tuple[Any,...] with read-only contents is the intended pattern; plan does not show __post_init__. Out of lane for deeper quality review — flagged for quality reviewer.
convention_verification.layer_discipline: Confirmed: plan states 'Pure redaction-layer work... L3 (alongside the composer tools)... no upward layer hops.' Composer code at src/elspeth/web/composer/ is L3. All imports in plan samples are from elspeth.web.composer.* (L3 intra-layer) or pydantic/stdlib.
convention_verification.audit_primacy_RedactionTelemetry: Plan lines 44/47 define RedactionTelemetry as a Protocol with OtelRedactionTelemetry (operational telemetry via structured-counter helper) and NoopRedactionTelemetry (test implementation). This is operational telemetry (ephemeral, real-time) as described in CLAUDE.md — not a logging substitute. Correctly positioned in the telemetry channel.
convention_verification.no_legacy_code: _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal is conditional ('if redundant, remove') rather than unconditional. Rev-3 N7 flagged this as 'should be unconditional'. Plan has not strengthened the language.

notes: Rev-4 reality check. All rev-3 BLOCKER B1 symbols and patterns are grounded: the property test now uses node.value_provider(args) from the TraversalNode API rather than an ad-hoc path.split('.') loop, and this is cross-verified by tests in test_walk_model_schema.py. All primary symbols cited in the plan (ToolArgumentError, _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS, tools.py dispatch dicts, _failure_result fall-through, redact_source_storage_path, REDACTED_BLOB_SOURCE_PATH, model_fields, model_validate) exist at the cited locations in the worktree. Pydantic 2.13.4 is installed (satisfies >=2.x). scripts/cicd/ exists; scripts/composer/ correctly does not exist. Rev-3 closure: B1 CLOSED (value_provider-based extraction), M1 CLOSED (walker floor-check in Task 10), M2 CLOSED (call-order pin in Task 17), W1/W2 PARTIAL (assertion rephrased to 'zero failures' but stale figure not refreshed), N3 OPEN (not listed in Appendix C; conditional removal language with no fix-up task for test_service.py:367). Three warnings raised: R-W1 (N3 test_service:367 unaddressed), R-W2 (W1/W2 partial), R-W3 (JSON-decode gate line-drift 1836 vs 1838). One minor: R-N1 (plan-file line numbers incorrectly cited as generated-file line numbers in property test docstring). The original B1 fix is structurally sound; the residual value_provider is None guard is a quality concern noted as R-N3 but is not a reality defect.
